mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Lower cost of capital directly increases present value of corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.41% as of April 2019 reflects recent Fed pivot toward accommodation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines do not significantly exceed baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates strengthening labor market and robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations. Positive employment momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 150,816k as of March 2019 reflects sustained job creation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods of rising PAYEMS do not exceed returns in periods of flat/declining PAYEMS (Mann-Whitney p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending and near-term economic momentum. Strong consumption growth triggers positive equity market sentiment and forward returns within 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side contraction. This predicts negative equity returns as investors anticipate earnings pressure and recession risk over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-side contraction or early recession signal
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. This joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% and PAYEMS at 150,816k as of March 2019
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (UNRATE × PAYEMS) produces Rank IC < 0.02 in absolute value over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with elevated consumer confidence (proxied by retail sales RSAFS) signals strong demand-side momentum and wealth effects. This joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as construction and consumption drive GDP growth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 2% month-over-month AND RSAFS rises > 0.3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,162k and RSAFS at 505,969M as of February 2019
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) signals monetary tightening regime. In this regime, further rate increases compress equity valuations and predict negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 254.148 and FEDFUNDS at 2.41% as of March 2019
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2% annualized signals late-cycle slowdown. In this regime, equity valuations compress as investors anticipate earnings pressure and recession risk, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
